Build generative AI applications with fo: Build generative AI applications with foundation models
Send a text prompt to an Amazon Bedrock: Send a text prompt to an Amazon Bedrock model.
Ask a Bedrock model a question about an: Ask a Bedrock model a question about an image.
Generate an image from a text prompt usi: Generate an image from a text prompt using Amazon Titan Image Generator or Stability AI models.
Generate vector embeddings from text usi: Generate vector embeddings from text using Amazon Titan Embed, Cohere Embed, or Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings models.
The foundation model to use for generati: The foundation model to use for generation.
The message or question to send to the m: The message or question to send to the model.
Instructions that set the model's behavi: Instructions that set the model's behavior and persona (e.g. "You are a helpful assistant that replies concisely.").
Controls how creative or predictable the: Controls how creative or predictable the response is. Lower values (e.g. 0.2) give focused, consistent answers. Higher values (e.g. 0.9) give more varied, creative output.
The maximum length of the response. One: The maximum length of the response. One token is roughly 4 characters. 2048 tokens ≈ ~1500 words.
Limits the pool of words the model picks: Limits the pool of words the model picks from (0–1). Lower values make responses more focused. Leave at 1 to disable this filter and use Temperature instead.
Text strings that, when generated, cause: Text strings that, when generated, cause the model to stop immediately (e.g. "END" or "\n\n"). Up to 4 sequences.
A unique name for this conversation's me: A unique name for this conversation's memory (e.g. "support-chat-user-123"). When set, the model remembers previous messages in this flow. Leave empty for a single-turn interaction with no memory.
Optionally include a file alongside your: Optionally include a file alongside your prompt. Choose whether to upload directly or reference one from S3.
The foundation model to use. Must suppor: The foundation model to use. Must support image input.
Choose how to provide the image — upload: Choose how to provide the image — upload directly or reference one already in S3.
What do you want the model to tell you a: What do you want the model to tell you about the image?
Instructions that guide the model behavi: Instructions that guide the model behavior.
Controls randomness. Lower values produc: Controls randomness. Lower values produce more deterministic output.
The maximum number of tokens to generate: The maximum number of tokens to generate.
The image generation model to use.
A text description of the image you want: A text description of the image you want to generate.
Describe what you do NOT want in the ima: Describe what you do NOT want in the image. Helps refine the output.
Image width in pixels. Must be supported: Image width in pixels. Must be supported by the model.
Image height in pixels. Must be supporte: Image height in pixels. Must be supported by the model.
A seed for reproducible results. Use the: A seed for reproducible results. Use the same seed and prompt to get the same image.
The text to generate embeddings for.
How the embeddings will be used — helps: How the embeddings will be used — helps the model optimize the output. Only applies to Nova Multimodal Embeddings models. Leave as "Generic Index" if unsure.
Size of the output vector. Larger = more: Size of the output vector. Larger = more accurate but slower/costlier. Titan Embed v2 supports: 256, 512, 1024. Nova Multimodal supports: 256, 384, 1024, 3072. Leave blank to use the model default.
Scale the output vector so its values ra: Scale the output vector so its values range between -1 and 1. Recommended for similarity comparisons. Only supported by Titan Embed v2.
